personas.name: Founder-CEO of an HR-heavy SG SME (Archetype A)
personas.icp: Owner-operator / managing director of a 50–150 staff SG SME in F&B groups, logistics, security services, retail, or B2B services. Has an HR exec doing 70% admin (contracts, onboarding, payroll prep, leave admin) and 30% strategic. Just lost an admin to retirement / pregnancy / a competitor and is staring at a S$3,800/mo replacement hire on top of 6.7% PMET wage inflation. A stranger reading this names: typical SBF / SCCCI member firm in the 80–120 staff band, 10–25 years old, family-influenced governance.
pains: Cannot afford another S$3,800/mo HR exec; the existing exec is at burnout and considering quitting
HR contracts and onboarding paperwork are templated in Word; every senior change takes 3+ days of cleanup
MOM COMPASS scoring on EP applications has tightened — last 2 EP renewals took >12 weeks
Tried Zapier / Make / ChatGPT informally; neither stuck because no one in the firm could maintain them
No clear sense of which workforce processes are JR+ fundable; would consider it if Elitez quoted a turnkey scope
personas.current_workaround: One overworked HR exec maintaining Word templates + Excel trackers + Talenox payroll + manual MOM/CPF filings; founder personally chases EP applications and answers HR-policy edge cases. Occasional ad-hoc S$2–4k engagements with a corp-sec firm or HR consultancy when something legal-adjacent comes up. ChatGPT Plus on the founder's personal account, used inconsistently.

nba.summary: Existing HR exec @ S$3,800/mo doing 8 hr/wk on contract + onboarding admin + ad-hoc HR consultant spend + ChatGPT Plus on the founder's account.

tooling_lines.tool: HR exec time on admin (32 hr/mo @ S$22/hr loaded)
ChatGPT Plus / Copilot on founder account
Founder time on EP / MOM filings (4 hr/mo @ S$80/hr loaded)

personas.name: Growth-stage founder with retention crisis (Archetype B)
personas.icp: Founder / co-founder of an 80–250 staff SG SME, post-Series-A, prepping for Series B or strategic exit within 12–24 months. Has a Group HR Director or Head of People newly hired (often the first People-function hire). Two of the last three senior hires left in <18 months. Term sheet from last round flagged 'key-person concentration risk.' A stranger names: Glints, Carro, Aspire, Endowus, Volopay, Sleek, or any DealStreetAsia 'Series B coming soon' SG headline at the people-org-investment moment.
pains: Senior staff churn at 25–35% annually; counter-offers from regional FAANG / Stripe / Anthropic offices outpace what cash can match
Career frameworks are missing — 'next year you'll be a senior X' is the entire career conversation today
L&D budget is fragmented across 3–4 vendors (Coursera enterprise, ad-hoc trainers, in-house ELM, peer-coaching); CFO can't justify spend
HCDT score (when last assessed) was sub-60; investors will ask about 'people maturity' at the next round
ESOP discussion is open but stalls because cap table is in a Google Sheet maintained by a co-founder
personas.current_workaround: Coursera / Udemy enterprise licenses (~S$8–15k/yr), an ad-hoc external L&D consultant (S$15–25k/yr), Lattice / 15Five for performance reviews (~S$5–8k/yr), a half-built career framework in Notion, and informal peer-mentoring led by the founders. ESOP exists in a Google Sheet, lawyered ad-hoc when grants are issued.

nba.summary: Coursera / Udemy enterprise + external L&D consultant + Lattice / 15Five + Group HR Director time on capability building + lawyer fees on ad-hoc ESOP grants.

tooling_lines.tool: Group HR Director time on capability work (12 hr/mo @ S$60/hr loaded)
Lawyer fees on ad-hoc ESOP grant work (~S$8k/yr)

personas.name: Mid-market SME owner doing 12-month transformation (Archetype C)
personas.icp: Owner / Group MD of a 150–500 staff SG SME, often a Gen-2 successor mid-handover, in manufacturing, healthcare clinic chains, F&B groups, security services, logistics. Pre-IPO or pre-PE-liquidity within 24 months. Has a CFO + Head of People + Head of Ops. JRET/HCDT baseline (if done) sits below 65; the board / family council has approved a 12-month workforce transformation budget. A stranger names: an established SBF/SCCCI Council Member firm in F&B / logistics / healthcare, 30–60 years old, second-generation in the chair.
pains: Workforce strategy is 'do what we did last year' — no operating model refresh in 5+ years; investor / board patience is finite
8–12 redesignable roles identified by recent consultancy diagnostic; nobody to project-manage the redesign through to in-production handover
Capital cycle pressure: must demonstrate measurable workforce productivity uplift in the next annual report
Multiple grants potentially available (JR+, EDG, SFEC) but the firm has no in-house grant lead — last EDG application took 14 weeks and was rejected
Tech vendor landscape is fragmented; the family wants 'one trusted partner' not 5 vendor relationships to manage
personas.current_workaround: Big-4 / boutique consultancy engagement (S$80–200k for the strategy / diagnostic phase, ad-hoc), in-house Head of People running capability building manually, fragmented HR/payroll/L&D SaaS stack, no HR analytics layer, no production tech for the redesigned roles. Grants applied for piecemeal by the CFO with high failure rate.

nba.summary: Big-4 / boutique consultancy engagement + Head of People time on capability + fragmented HR/L&D SaaS + CFO time on grant chasing + no production workforce-tech layer (requires net-new spend).

tooling_lines.tool: Big-4 / boutique strategy + diagnostic engagement (~S$100k/yr amortised)
Head of People time on capability + change mgmt (24 hr/mo @ S$80/hr loaded)
Fragmented HR/L&D SaaS stack (5–6 tools, ~S$25k/yr midpoint)
CFO time on grant applications (10 hr/mo @ S$120/hr loaded)
Workforce analytics gap (no current tooling — net-new spend if not us)

pricing_models.name: Pre-built workflow bundle (annual, per-firm flat)
pricing_models.rationale: Aligns with the 'sell engagements not products' implication. SG SME procurement preference is for single-fee turnkey engagements (BoardRoom Pulse 2024 + SBF Digital Pulse 2025). Maps directly to PSG (per-firm cap S$30k), JR+ (per-project cap S$50k), and EDG (per-project) grant arithmetic. The pre-built workflow library is the unit of sale, not seats or API calls.

pricing_models.name: Per-seat / per-stakeholder
pricing_models.rationale: Standard Zapier / Make / Lattice pattern. Penalises larger headcount which is exactly the wrong incentive for a workflow that should be deployed to the whole org. Also doesn't map to PSG's per-firm cap. SG SMEs reliably reject per-seat pricing once they realise the 80-staff scale-up math; recurring complaint in Indie Hackers SG threads.

pricing_models.name: Usage-based / per-API-call / per-workflow-run
pricing_models.rationale: Zapier task-based / OpenAI per-token pattern. SG SMEs hate the 'surprise bill' dynamic — public complaint pattern on Hardware Zone / Indie Hackers SG / Reddit r/singapore. Useful as a backstop for very high-volume workflows but never as the headline tier.

pricing_models.name: Subscription PSG-anchored monthly (≤S$500/mo)
pricing_models.rationale: PSG-effective entry tier for the self-serve / micro-SME wedge. S$300–500/mo lands inside the impulse-buy band (<S$10k/yr post-grant). Best for the 'first product' wedge before upgrading into a JR+ Archetype.

pricing_models.name: Outcome-based (success fee on JRET / HCDT delta)
pricing_models.rationale: Maximum alignment with the JR+ funding logic, minimum buyer comprehension. SG SME founders find it difficult to size and approve outcome-based commitments; useful as a secondary KPI in proposals (e.g. 'we commit to JRET +15') but not as the default pricing shape.

elasticity_heuristics.segment: Founder-CEO of an HR-heavy SG SME (Archetype A)
elasticity_heuristics.evidence: PSG-anchored: SG SME budget threshold for unfamiliar SaaS hardens at the S$30k PSG cap (effective S$15k after grant); JR+ Archetype A at S$30–50k gross lands at S$9–15k nett, exactly inside the band the founder will approve without board / family-council escalation. Above S$15k effective the founder defers or self-DIYs with an offshore VA + ChatGPT. Indie Hackers SG threads (2025-Q4) and SBF Digital Pulse 2025 confirm the S$15k effective ceiling repeatedly. Walks at S$15k+ effective.

elasticity_heuristics.segment: Growth-stage founder with retention crisis (Archetype B)
elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Investor / board pressure dominates pure price sensitivity but founders still anchor against the 'we have a People function now' budget line which usually opens at S$80–150k/yr loaded (HoP salary + 1–2 admin). A S$40–70k gross JR+ Archetype B at S$12–21k nett reads as 'a third of one People hire'; that's the zone where the conversation flips from price-led to value-led. Above S$70k gross, founders demand the IC-grade ROI worksheet and a multi-quarter rollout plan.

elasticity_heuristics.segment: Mid-market SME owner doing 12-month transformation (Archetype C)
elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Capital-cycle and family-governance pressure dominates. Big-4 / boutique consultancy engagements at S$80–200k are accepted as 'the price of getting the strategy right'; XinceAI Archetype C at S$80–150k gross + 70% JR+ subsidy lands at S$24–45k nett, which the family CFO frames as 'one-quarter the consultancy fee, plus the working tech.' Will pay above headline if the engagement includes a named Elitez Group senior partner and a JRET/HCDT outcome guarantee. Walks if pricing reads 'SaaS-tier-without-the-rigour' or if the named partner can't be guaranteed.

recommended_tiers.name: Archetype A · Workforce Modernisation Sprint
what_in: 3-month engagement (Diagnose / Redesign / Deploy)
JRET-compatible workforce assessment + Free + Full Diagnostic + AI Readiness Assessment
Job-role redesign workshop for 3–5 high-leakage roles with HR + line managers
Deploy: HR Contract Creation, Onboarding Automation, Resume Builder, C-Suite Dashboard (workforce analytics view)
MOM COMPASS-friendly job description auto-fill + CPF / IR8A reconciliation export
JRET pre/post measurement on 3–5 redesigned roles
WSG WDG(JR+) eligible (Component 1 + 3, 70% subsidy via BGP)
Founder-led setup; named CSM for the engagement duration
what_excluded: Career frameworks + L&D / training platform deployment (upgrade to Archetype B)
Multi-quarter capability transfer + onsite mentoring (upgrade to Archetype B)
Workforce strategy / operating model redesign (upgrade to Archetype C)
Custom workflow build outside the 9-product library (priced separately)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$30,000 list / ~S$9,000 nett after JR+ — less than 3 months of one HR exec at S$3,800/mo, and you ship 3 production workflows + a JRET-measured role redesign instead of one more pair of hands.

recommended_tiers.name: Archetype B · Talent Retention & L&D Stack
what_in: Everything in Archetype A, plus:
6-month engagement (Capability Phase 1 / Tech Deployment / Capability Phase 2)
Onsite consultant: HR/line-manager workshops, career framework design, KPI/skills taxonomy
Deploy: Staff Training Platform + ElixCraft (Gamified Lifecycle) + supporting HR workflows
HCDT pre/post measurement on L&D + career-development dimensions
Career frameworks documented for all redesigned roles
Train-the-trainer + KPI calibration during reinforcement phase
WSG WDG(JR+) eligible (Component 2 + 3, 70% subsidy via BGP)
SFEC stackable on the L&D / training portion (90% co-funding ceiling)
Dedicated CSM + monthly executive review
what_excluded: 12-month strategic workforce transformation + workforce strategy phase (upgrade to Archetype C)
Bespoke valuation / equity advisory (priced separately via Elitez-ESOP)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$50,000 list / ~S$15,000 nett after JR+ — a third of one Head of People hire, and you ship a documented career framework + L&D platform + HCDT score uplift the next investor will ask for.

recommended_tiers.name: Archetype C · Strategic Workforce Transformation
what_in: Everything in Archetype B, plus:
12-month strategic engagement (Strategy / Capability Building / Tech Deployment / Embedding)
Workforce strategy + JRET + HCDT baselines on Day 0; identification of 8–12 redesignable roles
Multi-month onsite mentoring + change management + train-the-trainer at scale
Full XinceAI workforce tech stack + Training Platform + ElixCraft + C-Suite (workforce analytics view) + reframed Market Insights (workforce skills market intel)
Workforce skills market intelligence — salary band + skills gap + talent supply for redesigned roles
Custom workflow build (1 included; further at S$8–15k/workflow)
WSG WDG(JR+) (Components 1 + 2 + 3, 70% subsidy) + EDG stackable on consulting + SFEC stackable on L&D
Named Elitez Group senior partner relationship for the engagement; quarterly board pack contribution
JRET uplift guarantee on 8–12 roles + HCDT improvement guarantee across IHRP dimensions
what_excluded: M&A transaction support + cap-table cleanup (priced separately via Elitez-ESOP)
Multi-country rollout (SG-only headline; MY/ID add-ons priced separately Q4 2026+)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$120,000 list / ~S$36,000 nett after JR+ — half the cost of one quarter of Big-4 consultancy, with the working tech in production, the JRET / HCDT proof artefacts the board needs, and a named Elitez Group senior partner on the relationship for 12 months.

grants.name: WDG(JR+) — Workforce Development Grant Job Redesign Plus
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding; project must be delivered by a WSG pre-approved JR+ consultant (Elitez Group is on the approved list, LOA valid through 17 Mar 2027); project must produce a measurable JRET / HCDT outcome AND demonstrate local-employee benefit per CFP spec p4.
applies_to_tiers: Archetype B · Talent Retention & L&D Stack

grants.name: PSG (Productivity Solutions Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, ≤200 employees AND ≤S$100M annual revenue, purchase for own business use; vendor must be IMDA pre-approved for the relevant solution category. Hard pre-req: XinceAI must apply for PSG pre-approval at IMDA in the multi-workflow / HR-finance-ops category — currently NOT listed (target submission Q3 2026).

grants.name: EDG (Enterprise Development Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, no headcount cap; project submitted via BGP before start under Innovation & Productivity, Process Redesign, or Human Capital Development pillar; SMEs in Tier-1 priority sectors (advanced manufacturing, F&B, retail digitalisation, professional services) get 70%. Applies to the consulting wrap bundled with the SaaS deliverable.
applies_to_tiers: Archetype B · Talent Retention & L&D Stack

grants.name: SFEC (SkillsFuture Enterprise Credit)
grants.eligibility: SG enterprise with ≥S$750 SDL contributions over qualifying period AND ≥3 SG local employees; stackable on top of PSG / JR+ / EDG for the training / L&D component of an engagement only, not the SaaS subscription itself. Extended to 30 Jun 2026 by Budget 2024.
applies_to_tiers: Archetype B · Talent Retention & L&D Stack
